core.label: F# Fundamentals
core.description: Syntax, types, immutability, and functional basics.

topics.label: .NET Setup & F# Interactive
topics.description: Install .NET SDK and use dotnet fsi (F# Interactive). F# runs on the .NET runtime.

topics.label: Let Bindings & Immutability
topics.description: Bind values with let (immutable by default). Use mutable only when necessary.

topics.label: Basic Types
topics.description: int, float, string, bool, char, unit. F# is statically typed with powerful type inference.

topics.label: Functions
topics.description: Define with let, currying by default, partial application, and function composition (>>).

topics.label: Pipe Operator (|>)
topics.description: Chain function calls for readable data pipelines. The pipe is F#'s most used operator.

topics.label: Tuples
topics.description: Group values with (a, b, c). Tuples are used for multiple returns and pattern matching.

topics.label: Lists, Arrays & Sequences
topics.description: Immutable lists [1;2;3], mutable arrays [|1;2;3|], and lazy sequences. Rich module functions.

topics.label: Pattern Matching
topics.description: match...with for exhaustive decomposition. Pattern matching is F#'s primary control flow mechanism.

topics.label: Records
topics.description: Immutable data types with named fields. Records have structural equality by default.

topics.label: Discriminated Unions
topics.description: Sum types with cases that can carry data. DUs model domain states precisely.

core.label: Type System & Error Handling
core.description: Option, Result, units of measure, and computation expressions.

topics.label: Option Type
topics.description: Some value or None. F# uses Option instead of null for absent values.

topics.label: Result Type
topics.description: Ok value or Error for railway-oriented programming. Functional error handling.

topics.label: Active Patterns
topics.description: Custom pattern matching with (|Pattern|) syntax. Extend pattern matching for your domain.

topics.label: Units of Measure
topics.description: Compile-time dimensional analysis with [<Measure>]. Prevent unit mismatch bugs.

topics.label: Modules & Namespaces
topics.description: Organize code with module and namespace. Modules contain values, namespaces contain types.

topics.label: Computation Expressions
topics.description: async { }, task { }, result { }. Computation expressions are F#'s monad abstraction.

topics.label: Async & Task
topics.description: Async workflows with async { } and .NET task interop with task { }.

topics.label: Type Providers
topics.description: Generate types from external data sources at compile time. CSV, JSON, SQL type providers.

core.label: OOP, Testing & .NET Integration
core.description: Classes, testing, and interop with the .NET ecosystem.

topics.label: Classes & Interfaces
topics.description: Define classes, implement interfaces, and use object expressions. F# supports OOP for .NET interop.

topics.label: C# Interop
topics.description: Call C# libraries from F# and vice versa. Full .NET interoperability.

topics.label: NUnit / Expecto
topics.description: Test F# with NUnit, xUnit, or the F#-native Expecto framework.

topics.label: FsCheck (Property Testing)
topics.description: Property-based testing inspired by QuickCheck. Generate random inputs to find edge cases.

topics.label: Domain Modeling
topics.description: Use DUs, records, and single-case unions for type-safe domain modeling. Make illegal states unrepresentable.

topics.label: Collection Pipelines
topics.description: List.map, Seq.filter, Array.fold with |> for data transformation. F#'s bread and butter.
